title: Child vs Children
wikitext:
[]: Child vs Children             Quote Post by Jay911 » Fri Mar 30, 2007 2:25 pm Apologies if this is an old topic.. my lurking here for months hasn't brought it up to me, and I don't know how you'd easily search for such a thing (as in, the search terms would come up in probably almost everything on the site). In the process of writing a story, I've been given grief by a test reader for using "First Children" to refer to Rei. I acknowledged that speaking strictly of grammar in the English language, he is correct; but that there is an insistence somewhere in the plethora of notes folks have collected re NGE that says that Children is the correct term. I can't find the assertion right now (and we can discuss why I'm putting stuff in a story when I don't have good reasons to back it up, later) but I seem to remember that Anno or someone connected to the writing was quoted as saying "Think of the meaning of the word CHILDREN". My response to that is "Uh doesn't it mean more than one child?" My question for EMF is, then, what is the "proper" appellation to refer to the chosen pilots for the Evangelions? "Nth Child" or "Nth Children"? If "Children", what is the reason behind it?

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Mar 30, 2007 2:41 pm Nothing springs to mind but I'd suggest that Anno et al would not make such a simple mistake and that they chose 'children' for a reason. but that there is an insistence somewhere in the plethora of notes folks have collected re NGE that says that Children is the correct term.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Mar 30, 2007 3:00 pm child children, I don't care. But the people that know more about Evangelion than me use Children, so I don't mind. It's children. Really.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 30, 2007 3:22 pm I never did any research on the topic, but I always chalked this one up to a case of Engrish. For what reason could Anno have chosen Children instead of Child? In English is makes no grammatical sense. I somehow doubt that non-native speakers of English could have deciphered some deeper meaning from the improper usage of "Children."

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 30, 2007 3:57 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: For what reason could Anno have chosen Children instead of Child?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 30, 2007 4:37 pm It's Engrishy. The original Japanese is "Children", which makes perfect sense when considering Rei and Kaworu, but I'm not sure if it's ever been stated that there was a reason behind the grammatically incorrect "Children". They changed it to "Child" in the anime, because according to Amanda Winn Lee, "Children" "sounded stupid". Viz remained faithful to "Children", which is more pressing, because Shinji comments early on in the manga that "Children" is awkward.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 30, 2007 4:37 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: For what reason could Anno have chosen Children instead of Child? In English is makes no grammatical sense.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Mar 30, 2007 6:52 pm WUTS SAUCE? seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Mar 30, 2007 7:02 pm Interweb speak for source, isn't it obvious the way she uses it?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 30, 2007 7:06 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The original Japanese is "Children", which makes perfect sense when considering Rei and Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 30, 2007 7:21 pm Reichu wrote: BrikHaus81 wrote: For what reason could Anno have chosen Children instead of Child? In English is makes no grammatical sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Mar 30, 2007 7:38 pm Besides the Engrishness and the plural of the fact there's more than one, I always thought it had some to do with the final scene (26), or vice-versa.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 30, 2007 7:51 pm Reichu wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: The original Japanese is "Children", which makes perfect sense when considering Rei and Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by Omegagouki » Fri Mar 30, 2007 7:52 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: They changed it to "Child" in the anime, because according to Amanda Winn Lee, "Children" "sounded stupid".

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 30, 2007 7:59 pm Omegagouki wrote: The Commentary of EVIL wrote: Taliesin Jaffe: And we set up for the frame of this entire film, which is this practice at the school, with all of our Children. The First Children, the Second Children, the Third Child, weâ€™re in English now. Amanda Winn Lee: Thatâ€™s right. In the original Japanese, they call it the First Children, instead of the First Child, Second Children, Third Children. So for those of you who wanted us to remain absolutely hardcore faithful to the original Japanese, Iâ€™m sorry, but that would have just sounded stupid. Taliesin Jaffe: Yeah, yeah, most WordPerfect spell checks are intelligent enough to change this, kind of got to go with WordPerfect occasionally.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Mar 30, 2007 8:00 pm Does anyone have a transcript of the Commentary of Evil? It'd be nice to have something to laugh at, I love a good comedy. AWL does NOT know wtf she's talking about, somebody needs to drag her out into an empty alley and beat her repeatedly with a baseball bat. From what I've heard, GAINAX KNOWS it's "Child" in English NOT Children, but the fact they lefr it pluar is obviously a reference to there's several pilots around. *sigh*

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Mar 30, 2007 8:02 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: This is probably the only time I will agree with AWL.

[]:             Quote Post by Omegagouki » Fri Mar 30, 2007 8:13 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Does anyone have a transcript of the Commentary of Evil? It'd be nice to have something to laugh at, I love a good comedy.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 30, 2007 8:29 pm BobBQ wrote: First, do not accept something only because AWL says it would have sounded stupid otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Mar 30, 2007 8:32 pm Ah, the power of Janglish strikes again!
